description: A curated project-wide research history: key commits, issue-tracked decisions, merged backports, and major post-freeze exploration lines. It is not a strict git DAG and it is not an exhaustive list of every transient helper branch.

nodes.title: Research Repo Bootstrap
nodes.summary: Initial commit turned the repo into a Humanoid-Gym-based research workspace with scripts, configs, docs, artifacts, and issue-driven planning.

nodes.title: Shared Metric Schema
nodes.summary: A single evaluation contract standardized task, smoothness, and constraint metrics so PPO, heuristic, and SC-PPO could be compared on one scale.

nodes.title: Bounded Heuristic Baseline Sweep
nodes.summary: A bounded action-rate sweep chose `action_rate = -0.0050` as the heuristic anchor, which later became the baseline candidate in protocol-repair work.

nodes.title: SC-PPO PID Mainline
nodes.summary: SC-PPO replaced reward shaping with a Jacobian-style local-sensitivity constraint and PID-Lagrangian updates, creating the repo’s main method line.

nodes.title: Frozen Formal Compare Collapse
nodes.summary: The frozen `64 envs x 400 iterations` formal compare collapsed Vanilla PPO and every heuristic row to checkpoint 0, exposing a protocol failure.

nodes.title: Protocol Repair Probe
nodes.summary: A repaired `512 envs x 200 iterations` probe broke universal collapse but only rescued seed23, proving the blocker was protocol, not just selector bugs.

nodes.title: Revised Heuristic Formal Anchor
nodes.summary: Extending the repaired protocol to `512 envs x 400 iterations` finally produced a task-valid 3-seed heuristic anchor at checkpoints `350 / 300 / 350`.

nodes.title: MuJoCo Aligned Mixed Reading
nodes.summary: Once MuJoCo replay was aligned to the revised heuristic anchor, SC-PPO lost its earlier partial-transfer wording and the repo adopted a mixed result.

nodes.title: PID-Limited Ablation
nodes.summary: A matched plain-dual control collapsed, so PID-Lagrangian stayed as the formal SC-PPO recipe without expanding into a full component attribution study.

nodes.title: SN Feasibility Diagnostic
nodes.summary: Spectral Normalization proved implementable and measurable, but every reduced-budget SN-only variant remained task-invalid and the branch closed negative.

nodes.title: Random-Stairs Transfer Failure
nodes.summary: Selected rough-terrain checkpoints from Vanilla PPO, heuristic, and SC-PPO all collapsed on the first stairs-only stress test.

nodes.title: Research Delivery Freeze
nodes.summary: After mainline closure, MuJoCo alignment, PID ablation, SN diagnostics, and stairs failure, `main` was frozen as an archival research handoff branch.

nodes.title: Random-Stairs Protocol Repair Family
nodes.summary: A post-freeze family of five bounded random-stairs repair lines, from composition moderation to decoupled stair-band rewriting, still ended in universal collapse and closed the small-repair reading.

nodes.title: Structured-Stairs Redesign
nodes.summary: The first topology-level random-stairs redesign replaced pyramid stairs with a bounded structured staircase, but the full 3-seed selected-checkpoint run still collapsed for every method.

nodes.title: Stair-Gate Redesign
nodes.summary: A second, more aggressive stair-gate redesign returned the robot to flat ground after a bounded obstacle, but even that semantic change did not move any selected checkpoint out of full collapse.

nodes.title: Anisotropic Constraint Diagnostic
nodes.summary: A bounded post-freeze anisotropic local-sensitivity branch repaired its own reporting loophole but still ended task-invalid, closing the shape-only repair direction and directly motivating objective-mismatch diagnosis.

nodes.title: Objective Mismatch Diagnostic
nodes.summary: Post-freeze instrumentation showed a repeatable tension: higher local-sensitivity cost often co-traveled with better task validity but rougher behavior.

nodes.title: Action-Rate Constraint Diagnostic
nodes.summary: A bounded action-rate-constrained SC-PPO branch first exposed threshold scale mismatch, then still collapsed after one calibrated follow-up, closing this objective-side replacement as another negative diagnostic.

nodes.title: SN Task-Stabilized Diagnostic
nodes.summary: A first-hidden-layer spectral-normalization stabilizer was layered onto task-valid SC-PPO 3.8, but the bounded single-seed feasibility run still remained fully collapsed and was closed without 3-seed promotion.

nodes.title: Behavior Trace Metrics
nodes.summary: Trace capture added LDLJ/SPARC-like metrics and showed independent smoothness signals can complicate, not simply confirm, the existing local-sensitivity story.

nodes.title: Action-Side Scaling Line
nodes.summary: A same-question action-side scaling replacement was closed negative under the canonical rough-terrain entry, so the repo moved on to a cleaner output-side variant instead of reopening nearby scaling schedules.

nodes.title: Output-Side Scaling Line
nodes.summary: A clean output-side scaling replacement line was opened after action-side scaling failed, but the canonical 3-seed run still missed the shared entry gate.

nodes.title: Orthogonal Actor Line
nodes.summary: Orthogonal actor parametrization plus bounded gain became the first architecture-line candidate, but both the main probe and gain-isolation follow-up collapsed.

nodes.title: LayerNorm Actor Line
nodes.summary: LayerNorm actor became the first architecture-side candidate to pass the Isaac internal challenge (selected=final=400 on 3/3 seeds via num_learning_epochs=3), but three other reliability levers closed negative and the MuJoCo replay showed ~5x worse joint acceleration and ~14x worse action jitter than SC-PPO 3.8, confirming it is not a smoothness replacement.

nodes.title: LayerNorm Reliability Probes
nodes.summary: Four bounded reliability levers were tested on the canonical entry: fixed schedule (collapsed), lower init noise (collapsed), lower desired_kl=0.005 (collapsed), and num_learning_epochs=3 (passed: selected=final=400 on all three seeds). Only the last lever cleared the Isaac internal challenge.

nodes.title: LayerNorm MuJoCo Cross-Engine Replay
nodes.summary: MuJoCo isaac_mainline replay of the epochs=3 candidate: fall_rate=0 but joint_acceleration ~5x worse than SC-PPO 3.8 and action_jitter ~14x worse, confirming the Jacobian constraint provides cross-engine smoothness robustness that LayerNorm architecture cannot replicate.

nodes.title: LayerNorm Actor Line Closure
nodes.summary: LayerNorm actor line closed negatively: while it achieved the first 3/3 selected=final=400 result in Isaac, the MuJoCo replay and Isaac smoothness degradation confirm it is not a smoothness replacement for SC-PPO 3.8. This further strengthens the evidence that the Jacobian/double-backward path is uniquely effective for cross-engine smoothness.

nodes.title: ScaledLinear TorchScript Fix
nodes.summary: ScaledLinear.forward was changed from super().forward() to F.linear() to resolve torch.jit.script compatibility, enabling MuJoCo export for all replacement-mechanism models that use scaled output layers.

nodes.title: Action Scaling MuJoCo Replay
nodes.summary: MuJoCo isaac_mainline replay: all three seeds collapsed (fall=1.0), joint_acceleration 12.7x worse than Isaac (1836 vs 144), action_jitter 8.3. Extreme cross-engine smoothness degradation.

nodes.title: Output Scaling MuJoCo Replay
nodes.summary: MuJoCo isaac_mainline replay: all three seeds collapsed (fall=1.0), joint_acceleration 4.1x worse than Isaac (500 vs 121).

nodes.title: Cross-Engine Degradation Analysis
nodes.summary: Complete cross-engine degradation table across 5 methods: SC-PPO (1.08x), heuristic (1.01x), LayerNorm (3.5x), Action Scaling (12.7x), Output Scaling (4.1x). Jacobian constraint and heuristic penalty are the only mechanisms that preserve smoothness across engines.

nodes.title: Jacobian Sensitivity Causal Chain
nodes.summary: Checkpoint-sweep analysis reveals LayerNorm needs 3x higher Jacobian sensitivity (10.7 vs 3.6) to achieve task stability, directly explaining the 3.5x MuJoCo degradation. SC-PPO's PID-Lagrangian keeps sensitivity at 3.6 throughout training.

nodes.title: Plain Dual Ascent (PPO-Lagrangian) Comparison
nodes.summary: Full 3-seed plain dual ascent comparison: seed11 succeeds (sel=fin=400, fall=0.0), seed23 collapses (sel=0). PID-Lagrangian provides cross-seed stability that plain dual ascent lacks.

nodes.title: SC-PPO epochs=3 Reliability Repair
nodes.summary: Applying num_learning_epochs=3 to SC-PPO 3.8 produced mixed results (seed11 improved, seed23 degraded), confirming Jacobian constraint interacts differently with training schedule than LayerNorm architecture.

nodes.title: LDLJ/SPARC Trace Comparison
nodes.summary: 20-episode trace comparison reveals smoothness as two-dimensional: LayerNorm wins on kinematic smoothness (LDLJ/SPARC), SC-PPO wins on dynamic smoothness (jnt_acc/jitter). The two mechanism families optimize different aspects of motion quality.
